name: Dragon
description: A striking heirloom carrot with deep purple-red skin and a bright orange interior. The anthocyanins in the skin provide a nutritional boost and the flavor is sweet and rich.
history: An ancient carrot type — purple was the original carrot color before Dutch breeders developed orange carrots in the 17th century. Dragon is a modern selection that highlights this ancestral trait.
seasonal_quirks: Purple skin color is most intense in cool conditions — summer heat can cause fading. Interior stays orange regardless. Flavor peaks after frost.
harvest_cues: Skin should be deep purple-red. Shoulders 3/4 inch or more. 75–80 days from seeding.
variety_notes: The purple skin doesn't penetrate to the interior — peeling removes the anthocyanins. Use unpeeled for maximum nutritional value.
preservation: Store in cold, moist conditions. Color fades somewhat when cooked.
